_note: ORIGINAL CS Navigator content added to align Learn with the existing Part 2 quiz category. No source prose, examples, or exercises were copied.
title: Algorithm Problems: Part 2
summary: Coordinate multiple values and define edge-case behavior.
refresher: A multi-step algorithm may track a value and its index or combine a count with a total. State what remains true after each pass, then handle empty input and ties deliberately.

blocks.body: A multi-step algorithm may track a value and its index or combine a count with a total. State what remains true after each pass, then handle empty input and ties deliberately. Coordinate multiple values and define edge-case behavior.

blocks.caption: The empty guard runs before indexing, and every later value can replace the candidate index.

blocks.title: Track the state
blocks.body: Without the empty case, the first access fails before the loop can establish any useful state.

blocks.prompt: Why begin the loop at one?
choices: index zero is already the candidate
blocks.why: The first element has already initialized the best candidate, so comparison starts with the next item.
An equal later value does not satisfy strict greater-than, leaving the earlier index unchanged.
